articles.title: Scrap NEET, allow states to fill medical seats based on Class 12 marks, CM Vijay writes to Centre
articles.description: Following the NEET-UG 2026 cancellation due to paper leak allegations, Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay urged the Union government to scrap NEET. He advocated for state-level admissions based on Class 12 marks for MBBS, BDS, and AYUSH seats, citing the exam's flaws and its disadvantage to rural and government school students.

articles.title: US Commerce Secy Howard Lutnick backs away from his Epstein 'blackmail' claim in interview with House committee
articles.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick faced questioning from House lawmakers regarding his past interactions with Jeffrey Epstein. Lutnick revised earlier statements about Epstein engaging in blackmail, calling it mere speculation. He downplayed several meetings with Epstein, including a 2005 visit that initially disturbed him and his wife. Democrats criticized Lutnick's testimony as evasive, with some calling for his resignation.

articles.title: Indian students face longer New Zealand visa delays as scrutiny rises
articles.description: Indian students applying for New Zealand visas are experiencing significant processing delays, with applications now taking an average of 30 working days, nearly double the time for Chinese applicants. While rejection rates have improved, these extended timelines are causing concern for students and immigration professionals, potentially impacting New Zealand's appeal as a study destination.

articles.title: ‘Entirely unfounded’: UAE denies reports of secret visit by Israel PM Netanyahu during Iran conflict
articles.description: The UAE has officially denied reports of a secret visit by Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu or an Israeli military delegation during the recent Israel-Iran conflict. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs stated that all UAE-Israel ties are transparent under the Abraham Accords, dismissing claims of unannounced arrangements.

The CBI has arrested five individuals, with investigations pointing to a leak originating in Nashik and spreading through coaching networks. Protests intensify nationwide demanding accountability.

While both Cardi B and Stefon Diggs remain private about their romance this time, his ex chef has raised eyebrows after accusing him of being “abusive” towards the Grammy winner.

articles.title: ‘Rocky Start’ For Trump In China? Viral Posts Claim Xi Jinping ‘Sent A Message’
articles.description: President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing for a high-stakes summit with Xi Jinping, but social media quickly exploded over one detail, Xi was nowhere at the airport. Viral posts called the moment a diplomatic snub, while Chinese social media mocked Trump with the nickname “Chuan Jianguo.” Despite a grand red-carpet welcome complete with military honors, critics claimed the visit was already off to a rocky start. Here’s what really happened during Trump’s dramatic China arrival.

articles.title: Putin Humiliates Trump Right Before Xi Meeting; Sets His Own Conditions For Ukraine Agreement
articles.description: Russia has publicly laid down what it calls the “basic condition” for a ceasefire in Ukraine — and it directly targets Kyiv’s control over occupied territory.Speaking to reporters, Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said President Volodymyr Zelenskyy must order Ukrainian forces to stop fighting and withdraw from Donbas and what Moscow now calls “Russian regions” for a ceasefire to take effect. Only after that, he said, can “full-scale and very complex” peace talks begin.

articles.title: Karnataka’s Hijab Ban, Court Battle And A Fresh U-Turn - Explained
articles.description: The Karnataka government has withdrawn the contentious 2022 uniform order that restricted the wearing of hijabs in schools. The new directive, issued by the Congress-led government, allows students to wear religious symbols, including hijabs, alongside school uniforms, provided their faces are visible.

articles.title: 'There Will Be A Disaster...': China's RED HOT Warning For Trump | Xi Aide Wang Yi Roars On Cam
articles.description: Ahead of the May 13-15 summit in Beijing, China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi struck a cautionary tone, warning that “misunderstanding and misjudgement” between Washington and Beijing could derail fragile stability at a critical moment. Urging a reset in tone, Wang said the two powers “cannot change each other, but can change the way we get along,” pitching the visit as an opportunity to rebuild “win-win cooperation” after months of friction.

articles.description: In a show of solidarity with Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s call to reduce non-essential foreign travel and conserve foreign exchange, Realtors' apex body CREDAI on Wednesday announced it is shifting its flagship annual convention, NATCON 2026, from Amsterdam to an Indian city.

articles.title: Russia Says Ties With India 'Impossible To Destroy', Promises Uninterrupted Energy Supply
articles.description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov assured that India's interests in Russian energy supplies "will not be affected" amid what he described as "unfair competition."

articles.title: Iran redefines Hormuz territory, ‘expands’ the strait tenfold just before Trump-Xi talks in China
articles.description: Iran issued a fresh warning to NATO hours before the Trump-Xi meeting, as Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi accused the U.S. of “dishonesty” and blamed Washington for blocking peace efforts. During talks with Norway’s deputy foreign minister in Tehran, Araghchi also hinted at a new legal framework for the Strait of Hormuz, raising fears of tougher shipping controls in the world’s most critical oil chokepoint. The warning came as Iran ruled out another round of peace talks unless trust-building conditions are met. Tehran’s latest remarks add more pressure ahead of Trump’s high-stakes Beijing visit and widening tensions across the Middle East.

articles.title: ‘If Gen Z In Nepal Could...’: Arvind Kejriwal Urges Youth To Demand Accountability Over NEET Leak Row
articles.description:
Former Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal on Wednesday called on India’s Gen Z to demand accountability in the alleged NEET-UG paper leak controversy, saying repeated exam leaks have shattered the trust of students preparing for competitive tests. Addressing a press conference, the former Delhi Chief Minister drew comparisons with youth-led political movements in neighbouring countries, arguing that Indian students also have the power to force systemic change.

He said that if young people in Bangladesh and Nepal could influence political developments, Indian youth could similarly push authorities to act decisively against those responsible for paper leaks.

Kejriwal Questions Effectiveness Of CBI Investigations

Kejriwal also raised doubts over the effectiveness of past investigations conducted by the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) into examination scams.

According to him, several earlier probes failed to ensure strict punishment for the accused, allowing similar incidents to continue recurring. He argued that handing over the current NEET leak case to the CBI would “achieve nothing,” alleging that repeated paper leak incidents since 2014 have damaged the future of millions of students across the country, as per reports.

Kejriwal further claimed that many of these alleged leaks took place in states governed by the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), intensifying the political confrontation over the issue.

ALSO READ: Supreme Court Halts Madras HC Order Barring TVK MLA Sethupathi From Floor Test

NEET-UG 2026 Cancelled After Massive Leak Allegations

The controversy escalated after the National Testing Agency (NTA) cancelled the NEET-UG examination conducted on May 3 following allegations that questions carrying nearly 600 marks had leaked before the test.

More than 22 lakh students had appeared for the medical entrance examination across 5,400 centres in 551 Indian cities and 14 international locations.

Investigators later uncovered what officials described as a nationwide cheating network allegedly involving coaching centres, hostel operators, impersonators, and paper solvers.

One of the cases under investigation involves a third-year BAMS student from Nashik who allegedly bought the leaked question paper for Rs 10 lakh before reselling it for Rs 15 lakh through courier channels.

Officials said the leaked material was used to create a 410-question “guess paper,” which reportedly contained nearly 120 actual NEET questions circulated through coaching centres, hostels, and paid WhatsApp groups.

The case has now been transferred to the CBI for further investigation.

Tamil Nadu Leaders Renew Opposition To NEET

The latest controversy has once again revived opposition to NEET in Tamil Nadu, where political parties have consistently criticised the entrance examination system.

Kamal Haasan condemned the alleged leak, saying the hard work of lakhs of students had been destroyed by “criminal conspiracies.”

He questioned who would take responsibility for the mental trauma faced by aspirants and observed that “not a single NEET exam has been conducted without allegations of malpractice.”

Haasan also reiterated demands for education to be shifted back to the State List.

Meanwhile, Saravanan Annadurai of the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) said the cancellation validated the party’s long-standing opposition to NEET.

He renewed demands to abolish the examination and argued that medical admissions should instead be based on Class 12 marks.

articles.title: Former minister, four-term MLA Madhav Lal Singh dies at 73 in Jharkhand
articles.description: Jharkhand Chief Minister Hemant Soren expressed grief over Singh's death and said he has directed officers to ensure the last rites are held with full state honours.

articles.title: Trump-Xi tango: Elon Musk, Jensen Huang join China trip as Washington pushes Beijing to ‘open up’ more
articles.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said late on Tuesday (May 12) he will ask Chinese President Xi Jinping to “open up” China during his summit with the leader, as CEOs from several major U.S. companies accompany him on the visit. Trump made the comment in a social media post while also pushing back on media reports about the guest list for the trip, saying Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang had in fact been invited. “CNBC incorrectly reported that the Great Jensen Huang, of Nvidia, was not invited ... In actuality, Jensen is currently on Air Force One,” Trump wrote, adding that he looked forward to meeting Xi “in a matter of hours” and would make opening China his “very first request.”

articles.title: Guru Pradosh Vrat May 2026: Date, timings, puja rituals and significance
articles.description: Devotees will observe the auspicious Guru Pradosh fast on May 14, 2026, dedicated to Lord Shiva. This significant observance, held on Trayodashi tithi, involves fasting from sunrise to sunset. The day, falling on a Thursday, is believed to bestow wisdom and worldly pleasures upon those who worship with devotion, offering prayers and performing rituals.

articles.description: False claims about vaccines, lockdowns and “manufactured outbreaks” are spreading rapidly online as experts warn that Covid-era misinformation networks are reactivating around the hantavirus outbreak.
